trigger: deleted bin with files / file in Premiere
notes: Eye=off — no policy is active. FS untouched. Plugin agnostic. If files remain in FS — they will be imported on next eye=on / Refresh.

trigger: deleted bin with files / file in Premiere
axis_B_paused (files w/o clips + plugin: YES (files in FS without clips + plugin failed to maintain coverage)
contract_violated: Coverage contract (Axis B): "eye=on promises bin reflects FS folder content"
plugin_response_advanced: eye stored → off (force-pause); manifest cleared for removed entries; FS-events queued (no auto-import to avoid retry-loop)
plugin_response_simplified (layer over a: Layer over: simplified.broken = true (via event-trigger contract — see parked-notes). Global autoimport for entire mode halted.
recovery_paths_advanced: (a) Refresh — manual re-import (manifest repopulates); (b) mode-toggle Adv→Simplified — same as Refresh + clear simplified.broken flag; (c) flip eye on in Adv — full policy restore
recovery_paths_simplified: (a) Refresh — manual re-import + clear simplified.broken; (b) mode-toggle Simplified→Adv→Simplified — same; (c) exit to Adv, flip eye manually, return
notes: Stored eye=off persists across (a)/(b) — only (c) restores policy. After (a)/(b) without (c) the next FS-event triggers pause-cycle again. Mediator: policy change requires explicit per-row act.

axis_A_drift (clips misplaced): depends (if cancel mid-batch — partial clips may exist somewhere; usually no)
axis_B_paused (files w/o clips + plugin: YES (plugin failed to complete the import job)
plugin_response_advanced: eye stored → off; manifest reflects partial state
recovery_paths_advanced: Refresh / mode-toggle / eye flip — same 3 paths

trigger: deleted bin with files / file in Premiere
axis_B_paused (files w/o clips + plugin: no (this is deliberate intent via 3-way handshake)
contract_violated: Mirror DEL contract — explicit 3-way handshake (eye=on + DEL=on + master=on)
plugin_response_advanced: timer (5s default) → if not cancelled → OS trash via §9 flow; row removed
plugin_response_simplified (layer over a: Impossible in Simplified — DEL column hidden (#41 spec). With effectively DEL=off falls into case #2.
signal_advanced: timer only visible in progress panel; LED state mirror-deleting (per §9). Countdown ring on DEL cell — PARKED (see parked-notes)
recovery_paths_advanced: Cancel in progress panel (DEL checkbox auto-unchecks + autoimport stops)
notes: Covered by §9. Row disappears (not Missing) — Axiom of asymmetric ambiguity. Mirror DEL fires per diff: if diff suspicious → import wins (see parked-notes safety hierarchy).

trigger: moved/renamed bin WITH CLIPS out of parent
axis_B_paused (files w/o clips + plugin: no (plugin didn't try import — nothing failed; dedup OK for existing)
contract_violated: Structure parity contract (Axis A): "clips for FS files must live in bin for the corresponding row"
plugin_response_advanced: orphan old bin (mark as "alien" where it moved); recreate empty bin in SoT position on next FS-event for row; Mirror DEL force-disabled for drifted row until restore (§9 structure-lock preserved)
plugin_response_simplified (layer over a: same as adv (drift = local issue; doesn't affect mode globally)
signal_advanced: 4px solid accentFill (dark blue, drifted-state) — cascade up to root
signal_simplified: same as adv (LED visible in Simplified). Toggle NOT red (mode keeps working).
recovery_paths_advanced: Magnet (move scattered clips into recreated bin)
recovery_paths_simplified: Magnet (if available in Simplified header — see parked-notes; else — exit to Adv → Magnet)
notes: Magnet = Axis A tool ONLY. Side-files in orphan bin preserved (mediator doesn't touch what isn't its own — Herder Bucket DROPPED). After Magnet → row → healthy.

axis_B_paused (files w/o clips + plugin: no (FS folder may be empty or contain files not yet imported)
plugin_response_advanced: orphan instantly; recreate bin in SoT position on next FS-event for row via autoimport; Mirror DEL not affected (nothing to trash)
resulting_state: healthy (row healthy, orphan bin sits where it moved)
recovery_paths_advanced: Refresh (recreate bin if FS has files); or file appears in FS — autoimport recreates (if eye=on)
notes: Sub-case of #4 for empty bin. No drift because nothing misplaced. Import triggers only files, not folders/bins.

trigger: moved file out of bin (Premiere drag to wrong bin)
contract_violated: Structure parity contract (Axis A) — sub-case of #4 at file level
signal_advanced: 4px solid accentFill (drifted, cascade up to root)
recovery_paths_advanced: Magnet (pull file back to correct bin)
notes: Sub-case of #4 file-level. In spec §1 demo distinguish bin-level vs file-level drift.

trigger: renamed file in bin (Premiere clip rename)
axis_A_drift (clips misplaced): no (cosmetic — Premiere clip name separate from FS file name)
notes: Premiere clip rename — user-cosmetic, not SoT. FS file name unchanged. Plugin agnostic.

trigger: added side-file to our bin (Premiere import outside SheepDog flow)
recovery_paths_advanced: n/a. Magnet does NOT eject side-files (Herder Bucket DROPPED). Side-file remains in bin untouched.
notes: Sub-level polish: subtle info indicator "bin contains side-files" (parked, see parked-notes).

trigger: deleted file in Finder (FS-side single file)
axis_B_paused (files w/o clips + plugin: no (Premiere itself shows offline for deleted file)
contract_violated: (no contract from plugin — file-level FS event)
plugin_response_advanced: row stays healthy. Folder itself alive — this is Premiere's problem with offline clip reference
recovery_paths_advanced: n/a from our side. User relinks clip in Premiere via native UI, or deletes clip from bin (then #2)
notes: Missing is a folder-level state, NOT file-level. Premiere handles offline file references natively. Plugin doesn't intervene at file-level.

trigger: drive offline / network share dropout / deleted folder / renamed folder in Finder
contract_violated: (no contract — environmental event). Triggered by Axiom of asymmetric ambiguity
plugin_response_advanced: row → missing (subtype: enoent / offline / eacces / other)
signal_simplified: 4px solid red + ⚠ indicator visible via STATE LED
recovery_paths_advanced: Relink (⌕) or × to clean stale config entry; reconnect drive
recovery_paths_simplified: Relink (⌕ — present in LNK column in Simplified) or × to clean
notes: Folder-level state. Per FS axiom: ambiguous → always Missing (could be user delete / offline / eacces). Retry strategy: offline = passive backoff; enoent = no retry.

trigger: user manually toggled eye=off in Advanced
plugin_response_advanced: manifest preserved (for possible restore); pending imports cancellable (if running — continue, no new ones)
plugin_response_simplified (layer over a: N/A (no per-row eye UI in Simplified)
signal_advanced: eye-closed glyph (existing); LBL-dot stays as set
notes: Differs from #2 — here explicit user act, no violation. Final observable state same as #2 stable phase, but reached deliberately.

trigger: user clicked × on healthy child row
plugin_response_advanced: row force-disabled; stored values preserved; ImportFiles ignored for row
plugin_response_simplified (layer over a: same as adv (× available in both modes)

trigger: partial manual import + watch folder add (some files already in Premiere in wrong bins via dedup)
axis_A_drift (clips misplaced): YES (clips misplaced — sitting in wrong bins, not ours)
axis_B_paused (files w/o clips + plugin: no (plugin tried — dedup is OK behavior, not failure)
plugin_response_advanced: manifest enriched with dedup-rejected entries (existing clipIDs); drift detected — clips somewhere in Premiere but not in ours
recovery_paths_advanced: Magnet (move clips from wrong bins into our target bin)
notes: Real QA case. Magnet expanded scope: pull scattered clips. Without drift signal — user blames plugin "didn't import", though clips are already in project somewhere.

trigger: autocancel due to non-dedup import failure (Premiere broke import)
plugin_response_advanced: eye stored → off; manifest reflects state at moment of failure; force-pause to prevent retry-loop
signal_advanced: eye-closed glyph; tooltip "unknown import failure — try relaunch premiere" (parked, see parked-notes)
notes: Real QA case. Already implemented as autocancel in MVP. Footer tip — parked for polish.

trigger: Renamed BIN in Premiere (label change without move)
axis_A_drift (clips misplaced): no (label = display setting; mapping via internal ID intact)
plugin_response_advanced: nothing (manifest mapping uses Premiere internal bin ID, not label; label = display attribute)
signal_advanced: nothing (in SOURCE NAME mode — default — display shows FS-derived name; custom label visible in BIN NAME mode via RMB on column header)
notes: Custom labeling allowed. Column header is "SOURCE NAME" (default mode). RMB on header → context menu → toggle "BIN NAME" mode → display switches to Premiere's bin label. LMB on header = sort (standard). Plugin internally always knows both names. See parked-notes "Source/Bin Name display toggle" for UX contract.

trigger: empty bin in Premiere with eye=on (user deleted clip-references)
axis_B_paused (files w/o clips + plugin: YES (files in FS without clips + plugin failure)
notes: Identical to case #2 — partial vs full bin clearing — no functional difference.
